tu.prop: The general objective of this study is to clear up the relative importance of prosody in the identification of a foreign accent.

tuv.seg: Role of segmental and suprasegmental cues in the perception of maghrebian-acented French
Rôle du segmental et du suprasegmental dans la perception de l'accent maghrébin en français

tu.prop: Role of segmental and suprasegmental cues in the perception of maghrebian-acented French
The methodology we propose based on the prosody transplantation paradigm can be applied to different languages or language varieties.

tuv.seg: The general objective of this study is to clear up the relative importance of prosody in the identification of a foreign accent.
L'objectif général de cette étude est d'élucider l'importance relative de la prosodie dans l'identification d'un accent étranger.

tu.prop: The general objective of this study is to clear up the relative importance of prosody in the identification of a foreign accent.
Here it is applied to Magrhrebianaccented French: we wanted to study what is perceived when the segmental and suprasegmental characteristics of Maghrebian and native French speakers are crossed.

tuv.seg: The methodology we propose based on the prosody transplantation paradigm can be applied to different languages or language varieties.
La méthodologie que nous proposons, fondée sur le paradigme de transplantation de prosodie, peut s'appliquer à différentes langues ou variétés de langues.

tu.prop: The methodology we propose based on the prosody transplantation paradigm can be applied to different languages or language varieties.

Results obtained with French listeners (accent degree rating task) and Algerian listeners (origin identification task) converge and suggest that the articulation of phonemes overrides prosody to account for Maghrebian accents in French.

tuv.seg: Here it is applied to Magrhrebianaccented French: we wanted to study what is perceived when the segmental and suprasegmental characteristics of Maghrebian and native French speakers are crossed.

Ici elle est appliquée au français parlé avec l'accent maghrébin : nous voulions étudier ce qui est perçu quand les caractéristiques segmentales et suprasegmentales de Magrhébins et de locuteurs natifs du français sont croisées.

tu.prop: Here it is applied to Magrhrebianaccented French: we wanted to study what is perceived when the segmental and suprasegmental characteristics of Maghrebian and native French speakers are crossed.

tuv.seg: Results obtained with French listeners (accent degree rating task) and Algerian listeners (origin identification task) converge and suggest that the articulation of phonemes overrides prosody to account for Maghrebian accents in French.

Les résultats obtenus avec des auditeurs français (tâche d'évaluation du degré d'accent) et des auditeurs algériens (tâche d'identification de l'origine) sont convergents et suggèrent que l'articulation des phonèmes prime sur la prosodie pour rendre compte de l'accent maghrébin en français.

tu.prop: Results obtained with French listeners (accent degree rating task) and Algerian listeners (origin identification task) converge and suggest that the articulation of phonemes overrides prosody to account for Maghrebian accents in French.
What is commonly meant by "foreign accent" results from the confrontation of two phonological systems in contact -from a mother tongue (L1) and a target language (L2).

tuv.seg: Introduction
Introduction

tu.prop: Beyond the relations which exist between these languages a number of psycho-and socio-linguistic factors may come into consideration (even in the rating of a foreign accent).

tuv.seg: What is commonly meant by "foreign accent" results from the confrontation of two phonological systems in contact -from a mother tongue (L1) and a target language (L2).
Ce qui est communément appelé "accent étranger" résulte de la confrontation de deux systèmes phonologiques en contact -d'une langue maternelle (L1) et d'une langue cible (L2).

tu.prop: What is commonly meant by "foreign accent" results from the confrontation of two phonological systems in contact -from a mother tongue (L1) and a target language (L2).
This article is devoted to Maghrebian-accented French (spoken by Arabic or Berber natives).

tuv.seg: Beyond the relations which exist between these languages a number of psycho-and socio-linguistic factors may come into consideration (even in the rating of a foreign accent).
Outre les relations qu'entretiennent ces langues, un certain nombre de facteurs psycho-et sociolinguistiques peuvent entrer en jeu (même dans l'évaluation d'un accent étranger).

tu.prop: Beyond the relations which exist between these languages a number of psycho-and socio-linguistic factors may come into consideration (even in the rating of a foreign accent).
Is it by chance that the term accent has been chosen to designate both a prosodic prominence and a foreign way of speaking?

tuv.seg: This article is devoted to Maghrebian-accented French (spoken by Arabic or Berber natives).
Cet article est consacré au français à l'accent maghrébin (parlé par des locuteurs natifs de arabes ou berbères).

tu.prop: This article is devoted to Maghrebian-accented French (spoken by Arabic or Berber natives).
This question also applies to regional and social accents and some authors such as Rossi seem to answer it in the negative.

tuv.seg: Is it by chance that the term accent has been chosen to designate both a prosodic prominence and a foreign way of speaking?
Est-ce un hasard si le terme accent a été choisi pour désigner à la fois une prosodie marquée et une parlure étrangère ?

tu.prop: Is it by chance that the term accent has been chosen to designate both a prosodic prominence and a foreign way of speaking?
Invariants probably exist beyond regional differences between a Parisian and a person from Marseilles speaking English.

tuv.seg: This question also applies to regional and social accents and some authors such as Rossi seem to answer it in the negative.
À cette question qui s'applique également aux accents régionaux et sociaux, certains auteurs comme M. Rossi semblent répondre par la négative

tu.prop: This question also applies to regional and social accents and some authors such as Rossi seem to answer it in the negative.

If the information carried by the segmental content (properties linked to the sound patterns) often enables us to identify a specific accent prosody is also essential in language acquisition and may trigger certain adjustments during second language learning.

tuv.seg: Invariants probably exist beyond regional differences between a Parisian and a person from Marseilles speaking English.
Il existe sans doute des invariants au-delà des différences régionales entre un Parisien et un Marseillais parlant anglais.

tu.prop: Invariants probably exist beyond regional differences between a Parisian and a person from Marseilles speaking English.

Although the characteristic categories of the mother tongue prosody are extracted very early by infants (prior to the lexicon acquisition ) prosody is one of the most ticklish elements to teach in a second language.

tuv.seg: If the information carried by the segmental content (properties linked to the sound patterns) often enables us to identify a specific accent prosody is also essential in language acquisition and may trigger certain adjustments during second language learning.

Si l'information portée par le contenu segmental (les propriétés liées à la structure des sons) permet souvent d'identifier un accent spécifique la prosodie est également essentielle dans l'acquisition du langage et peut conditionner certains ajustements dans l'apprentissage d'une langue étrangère.

tu.prop: If the information carried by the segmental content (properties linked to the sound patterns) often enables us to identify a specific accent prosody is also essential in language acquisition and may trigger certain adjustments during second language learning.
If phonemic characteristics and prosodic dimensions are important a foreign accent should reflect both segmental and suprasegmental difficulties.

tuv.seg: Although the characteristic categories of the mother tongue prosody are extracted very early by infants (prior to the lexicon acquisition ) prosody is one of the most ticklish elements to teach in a second language.

Bien que les catégories caractéristiques de la prosodie de la langue maternelle soient extraites très tôt chez les nourrissons (avant l'acquisition du lexique), la prosodie est l'un des éléments les plus délicats à enseigner dans une langue seconde.

tu.prop: Although the characteristic categories of the mother tongue prosody are extracted very early by infants (prior to the lexicon acquisition ) prosody is one of the most ticklish elements to teach in a second language.
Recent studies thus approached the contribution of prosody to the perception of a foreign accent [5; 6].

tuv.seg: If phonemic characteristics and prosodic dimensions are important a foreign accent should reflect both segmental and suprasegmental difficulties.
Si les caractéristiques phonémiques et les dimensions prosodiques sont importantes, un accent étranger devrait refléter des difficultés à la fois segmentales et suprasegmentales.

tu.prop: If phonemic characteristics and prosodic dimensions are important a foreign accent should reflect both segmental and suprasegmental difficulties.
Likewise some investigations brought to light the important or even major role of prosody in the identification of regional accents or dialectal varieties [7; 8].

tuv.seg: Recent studies thus approached the contribution of prosody to the perception of a foreign accent [5; 6].
De récentes études ont ainsi abordé la contribution de la prosodie à la perception d'un accent étranger [5 ; 6].

tu.prop: Recent studies thus approached the contribution of prosody to the perception of a foreign accent [5; 6].

The TIE network (Tone and Intonation in Europe) in particular aims at providing a platform to coordinate these prosodic analyses of European languages and non-standard varieties (including immigrant communities) in the laboratory phonology approach.

tuv.seg: Likewise some investigations brought to light the important or even major role of prosody in the identification of regional accents or dialectal varieties [7; 8].
De même des travaux ont mis en évidence le rôle important voire majeur de la prosodie dans l'identification d'accents régionaux ou de variétés dialectales [7 ; 8].

tu.prop: Likewise some investigations brought to light the important or even major role of prosody in the identification of regional accents or dialectal varieties [7; 8].
A number of studies especially within the framework of the IViE project (Intonational Variation in English) revealed how certain intonational patterns are specific to some English varieties.

tuv.seg: The TIE network (Tone and Intonation in Europe) in particular aims at providing a platform to coordinate these prosodic analyses of European languages and non-standard varieties (including immigrant communities) in the laboratory phonology approach.

En particulier, le réseau TIE (Tone and Intonation in Europe) a pour objectif de fournir une plateforme pour coordonner ces analyses prosodiques des langues européennes et des variétés non standard - y compris des communautés d'immigrés -selon l'approche de la phonétique de laboratoire.

tu.prop: The TIE network (Tone and Intonation in Europe) in particular aims at providing a platform to coordinate these prosodic analyses of European languages and non-standard varieties (including immigrant communities) in the laboratory phonology approach.

The AMPER linguistic atlas of Romance languages considers French among others: it is a continuation of a series of inquiries concerning different stress patterns in patois from the North/North-East of France and more generally from the whole French-speaking area [12; 13].

tuv.seg: A number of studies especially within the framework of the IViE project (Intonational Variation in English) revealed how certain intonational patterns are specific to some English varieties.
De nombreuses recherches notamment dans le cadre du projet IViE (Intonational Variation in English) ont révélé combien certains patrons intonatifs sont spécifiques à certaines variétés d'anglais.

tu.prop: A number of studies especially within the framework of the IViE project (Intonational Variation in English) revealed how certain intonational patterns are specific to some English varieties.
To complete the survey let us cite a few additional studies on the identification of Arabic dialects [14; 15].

tuv.seg: The AMPER linguistic atlas of Romance languages considers French among others: it is a continuation of a series of inquiries concerning different stress patterns in patois from the North/North-East of France and more generally from the whole French-speaking area [12; 13].

L'Atlas Linguistique AMPER des langues romanes considère entre autres le français : il fait suite à une série d'enquêtes concernant différentes manifestations accentuelles dans les patois du Nord/Nord-Est de la France et plus généralement dans toute la zone francophone [12 ; 13].

tu.prop: The AMPER linguistic atlas of Romance languages considers French among others: it is a continuation of a series of inquiries concerning different stress patterns in patois from the North/North-East of France and more generally from the whole French-speaking area [12; 13].
The latter study was carried out using procedures put forth in Ramus's dissertation on the different rhythmic classes -especially syllable-timed vs. stress-timed.

tuv.seg: To complete the survey let us cite a few additional studies on the identification of Arabic dialects [14; 15].
Pour compléter l'étude, citons quelques études supplémentaires sur l'identification des dialectes arabes [14 ; 15].

tu.prop: To complete the survey let us cite a few additional studies on the identification of Arabic dialects [14; 15].
Others attempt to elucidate the role of intonation rather than rhythm (TIE and IViE projects in particular).

tuv.seg: The latter study was carried out using procedures put forth in Ramus's dissertation on the different rhythmic classes -especially syllable-timed vs. stress-timed.
Cette dernière étude utilise une procédure proposée dans la thèse de Ramus sur les différentes classes rythmiques, en particulier le rythme syllabique et le rythme accentué.

tu.prop: The latter study was carried out using procedures put forth in Ramus's dissertation on the different rhythmic classes -especially syllable-timed vs. stress-timed.
However Arabic and Berber prosody has barely been tackled and remains somewhat of an unexplored field -not to mention analyses of Arabs and Berbers speaking French.

tuv.seg: Others attempt to elucidate the role of intonation rather than rhythm (TIE and IViE projects in particular).
D'autres tentent d'élucider le rôle de l'intonation plutôt que celui du rythme (en particulier les projets TIE et IViE).

tu.prop: Others attempt to elucidate the role of intonation rather than rhythm (TIE and IViE projects in particular).

With over 900000 adult speakers living in Metropolitan France Arabic is nonetheless the main language other than French spoken in France before Alsacian (600000 speakers) according to a recent inquiry.

tuv.seg: However Arabic and Berber prosody has barely been tackled and remains somewhat of an unexplored field -not to mention analyses of Arabs and Berbers speaking French.
Cependant, la prosodie des parlers arabes et berbères a à peine été abordée et reste un peu un domaine inexploré, sans parler des analyses des Arabes et Berbères parlant français.

tu.prop: However Arabic and Berber prosody has barely been tackled and remains somewhat of an unexplored field -not to mention analyses of Arabs and Berbers speaking French.
For a discussion on Berber and in particular on Kabyle (Algerian Berber) one can refer to.

tuv.seg: With over 900000 adult speakers living in Metropolitan France Arabic is nonetheless the main language other than French spoken in France before Alsacian (600000 speakers) according to a recent inquiry.

Avec plus de 900 000 locuteurs adultes vivant en métropole l'arabe est pourtant, d'après une enquête récente, la principale langue autre que le français parlée en France devant l'alsacien (600 000 locuteurs).

tu.prop: With over 900000 adult speakers living in Metropolitan France Arabic is nonetheless the main language other than French spoken in France before Alsacian (600000 speakers) according to a recent inquiry.
For Arabic and in particular Moroccan Arabic see.

tuv.seg: For a discussion on Berber and in particular on Kabyle (Algerian Berber) one can refer to.
Pour une discussion sur le berbère et en particulier sur le kabyle (berbère d'Algérie) on pourra se reporter à.

tu.prop: For a discussion on Berber and in particular on Kabyle (Algerian Berber) one can refer to.
In our study the first objective of which is to appreciate what constitutes the Maghrebian accent 20 or so native speakers of Arabic Kabyle and French were recorded.

tuv.seg: For Arabic and in particular Moroccan Arabic see.
Pour l'arabe et en particulier l'arabe marocain voir.

tu.prop: For Arabic and in particular Moroccan Arabic see.
A perceptual study was conducted to sort out what is perceived when the segmental and suprasegmental features of the different speakers are crossed.

tuv.seg: In our study the first objective of which is to appreciate what constitutes the Maghrebian accent 20 or so native speakers of Arabic Kabyle and French were recorded.
Pour notre étude, dont le premier objectif est d'apprécier ce qui constitue l'accent maghrébin, une vingtaine de locuteurs arabes kabyles et français ont été enregistrés.

tu.prop: In our study the first objective of which is to appreciate what constitutes the Maghrebian accent 20 or so native speakers of Arabic Kabyle and French were recorded.
The proposed experiment could be replicated with other L1-L2 pairs and may find applications in automatic language/dialect identification.

tuv.seg: A perceptual study was conducted to sort out what is perceived when the segmental and suprasegmental features of the different speakers are crossed.
Une étude perceptive a été menée pour discerner ce qui est perçu lorsqu'on croise les caractéristiques segmentales et suprasegmentales des différents locuteurs.

tu.prop: A perceptual study was conducted to sort out what is perceived when the segmental and suprasegmental features of the different speakers are crossed.
It is described in next section with an accent degree evaluation task performed by French listeners and an origin identification task performed by Algerian listeners.

tuv.seg: The proposed experiment could be replicated with other L1-L2 pairs and may find applications in automatic language/dialect identification.
L'expérience proposée pourrait être reproduite avec d'autres paires L1-L2 et trouver des applications dans l'identification automatique de langues/dialectes.

tu.prop: The proposed experiment could be replicated with other L1-L2 pairs and may find applications in automatic language/dialect identification.
Results are provided in section 3: they suggest that the articulation of phonemes and voice quality take the first place as compared to prosody to identify a Maghrebian accent in French.

tuv.seg: It is described in next section with an accent degree evaluation task performed by French listeners and an origin identification task performed by Algerian listeners.
Elle est décrite dans la section suivante avec une tâche d'évaluation du degré d'accent auprès d'auditeurs français et une tâche d'identification de l'origine auprès d'auditeurs algériens.

tu.prop: It is described in next section with an accent degree evaluation task performed by French listeners and an origin identification task performed by Algerian listeners.

tuv.seg: Results are provided in section 3: they suggest that the articulation of phonemes and voice quality take the first place as compared to prosody to identify a Maghrebian accent in French.
Les résultats sont fournis à la section 3 : ils suggèrent que l'articulation des phonèmes et la qualité de la voix priment sur la prosodie pour identifier un accent maghrébin en français.

tu.prop: Results are provided in section 3: they suggest that the articulation of phonemes and voice quality take the first place as compared to prosody to identify a Maghrebian accent in French.

tuv.seg: Experiment
Expérience

tu.prop: We instructed 18 speakers to read a 400 word text: 6 Arab (2 Moroccan 2 Algerian and 2 Tunisian) 6 Kabyle and 6 French speakers living in the Paris region.

tuv.seg: Material
Matériel

tu.prop: The subjects were 25 years old on average and the Maghrebians had arrived in France for 2 years on average.

tuv.seg: We instructed 18 speakers to read a 400 word text: 6 Arab (2 Moroccan 2 Algerian and 2 Tunisian) 6 Kabyle and 6 French speakers living in the Paris region.
Nous avons fait lire un texte de près de 400 mots à 18 locuteurs : 6 Arabes (2 Marocains, 2 Algériens et 2 Tunisiens) 6 Kabyles et 6 Français vivant en région parisienne.

tu.prop: We instructed 18 speakers to read a 400 word text: 6 Arab (2 Moroccan 2 Algerian and 2 Tunisian) 6 Kabyle and 6 French speakers living in the Paris region.
Six other speakers of these different origins were recorded for a learning phase.

tuv.seg: The subjects were 25 years old on average and the Maghrebians had arrived in France for 2 years on average.
Les sujets étaient en moyenne âgés de 25 ans et les Maghrébins étaient en moyenne arrivés depuis deux ans en France.

tu.prop: The subjects were 25 years old on average and the Maghrebians had arrived in France for 2 years on average.
All of them were students or young doctors and there were as many males as females in each group.

tuv.seg: Six other speakers of these different origins were recorded for a learning phase.
Six autres locuteurs de ces différentes origines ont été enregistrés pour une phase d'apprentissage.

tu.prop: Six other speakers of these different origins were recorded for a learning phase.
The speakers had no precise idea on the present study.

tuv.seg: All of them were students or young doctors and there were as many males as females in each group.
Tous étaient étudiants ou jeunes docteurs et il y avait autant d'hommes que de femmes dans chaque groupe.

tu.prop: All of them were students or young doctors and there were as many males as females in each group.

They were requested to read the text of the PFC project (Phonology of Contemporary French) which was split into sentences: this way if they came up against a sentence or hesitated they would resume the reading from the beginning of the sentence.

tuv.seg: The speakers had no precise idea on the present study.
Les locuteurs n'avaient aucune idée précise sur l'étude en cours.

tu.prop: The speakers had no precise idea on the present study.
The recordings took place in Orsay in a soundproof booth with a high-quality microphone located about 20 cm from the mouth and were digitised at 22.05 kHz 16 bits mono.

tuv.seg: They were requested to read the text of the PFC project (Phonology of Contemporary French) which was split into sentences: this way if they came up against a sentence or hesitated they would resume the reading from the beginning of the sentence.

Il leur a été demandé de lire le texte du projet PFC (Phonologie du Français Contemporain ) qui a été divisé en phrases : ainsi s'ils butaient sur une phrase ou s'ils avaient une hésitation ils reprenaient la lecture au début de la phrase.

tu.prop: They were requested to read the text of the PFC project (Phonology of Contemporary French) which was split into sentences: this way if they came up against a sentence or hesitated they would resume the reading from the beginning of the sentence.
We extracted a dozen sentence pairs (the ones stemming from Maghrebian speakers the others from French speakers or Maghrebian speakers of different origins).

tuv.seg: The recordings took place in Orsay in a soundproof booth with a high-quality microphone located about 20 cm from the mouth and were digitised at 22.05 kHz 16 bits mono.
Les enregistrements effectués à Orsay en chambre sourde avec un microphone de haute qualité situé à environ 20 cm de la bouche ont été digitalisés à 22,05 kHz 16 bits mono.

tu.prop: The recordings took place in Orsay in a soundproof booth with a high-quality microphone located about 20 cm from the mouth and were digitised at 22.05 kHz 16 bits mono.
Energy was normalised and the segmental and suprasegmental characteristics of the different populations were crossed.

tuv.seg: We extracted a dozen sentence pairs (the ones stemming from Maghrebian speakers the others from French speakers or Maghrebian speakers of different origins).
Nous avons extrait une douzaine de paires de phrases (les unes provenant de locuteurs maghrébins, les autres de locuteurs français ou de locuteurs maghrébins d'origines différentes).

tu.prop: We extracted a dozen sentence pairs (the ones stemming from Maghrebian speakers the others from French speakers or Maghrebian speakers of different origins).
The sentences were selected on the basis of 3 criteria: presence of presumed typical (supra)segmental feature as in previous studies ;

tuv.seg: Energy was normalised and the segmental and suprasegmental characteristics of the different populations were crossed.
L'énergie a été normalisée et les caractéristiques segmentales et suprasegmentales des différentes populations ont été croisées.

tu.prop: Energy was normalised and the segmental and suprasegmental characteristics of the different populations were crossed.
matching realisations of liaisons and schwas;

tuv.seg: The sentences were selected on the basis of 3 criteria: presence of presumed typical (supra)segmental feature as in previous studies ;
Les phrases ont été sélectionnées sur la base de 3 critères : présence de traits (supra)segmentaux présumés typiques comme dans des études antérieures ;

tu.prop: The sentences were selected on the basis of 3 criteria: presence of presumed typical (supra)segmental feature as in previous studies ;
matching realisation of pauses and pitch -even if we can play on the latter parameters.

tuv.seg: matching realisations of liaisons and schwas;
réalisations de liaisons et de schwas concordantes ;

tu.prop: matching realisations of liaisons and schwas;
The sentences which ranged from 2.4 to 15.7 seconds were 8.7 seconds long on an average.

tuv.seg: matching realisation of pauses and pitch -even if we can play on the latter parameters.
réalisations des pauses et hauteur mélodique concordantes -même si on peut jouer sur ces derniers paramètres.

tu.prop: matching realisation of pauses and pitch -even if we can play on the latter parameters.

tuv.seg: The sentences which ranged from 2.4 to 15.7 seconds were 8.7 seconds long on an average.
Les phrases qui allaient de 2,4 à 15,7 secondes étaient en moyenne de 8,7 secondes.

tu.prop: The sentences which ranged from 2.4 to 15.7 seconds were 8.7 seconds long on an average.

In order to copy the prosodic parameters from a speaker to another one a script was written for the PRAAT software ( http://www.fon.hum.uva.nl/praat/ ) which enables signal handling and re-synthesis with the help of the PSOLA algorithm.

tuv.seg: Prosody transplantation: methodology
Transplantation de prosodie : méthode

tu.prop: It follows the upcoming scheme: extraction and then transplantation phoneme by phoneme of durations and then pitch.

tuv.seg: In order to copy the prosodic parameters from a speaker to another one a script was written for the PRAAT software ( http://www.fon.hum.uva.nl/praat/ ) which enables signal handling and re-synthesis with the help of the PSOLA algorithm.

Afin de recopier les paramètres prosodiques d'un locuteur sur un autre un script a été écrit pour le logiciel PRAAT (http://www.fon.hum.uva.nl/praat/) qui permet de manipuler et resynthétiser le signal de parole à l'aide de l'algorithme PSOLA.

tu.prop: In order to copy the prosodic parameters from a speaker to another one a script was written for the PRAAT software ( http://www.fon.hum.uva.nl/praat/ ) which enables signal handling and re-synthesis with the help of the PSOLA algorithm.
Before starting on this phase a segmentation into phonemes is required: it was made by an expert under PRAAT on the basis of perceptual and visual clues -on spectrograms.

tuv.seg: It follows the upcoming scheme: extraction and then transplantation phoneme by phoneme of durations and then pitch.
Il suit le schéma suivant : extraction puis transplantation phonème par phonème des durées puis de la hauteur.

tu.prop: It follows the upcoming scheme: extraction and then transplantation phoneme by phoneme of durations and then pitch.
The analysis of phoneme durations pauses and fundamental frequency (F 0 ) is then automatic.

tuv.seg: Before starting on this phase a segmentation into phonemes is required: it was made by an expert under PRAAT on the basis of perceptual and visual clues -on spectrograms.
Avant d'entamer cette phase une segmentation en phonèmes s'impose : elle a été effectuée manuellement par un expert sous PRAAT sur la base d'indices perceptifs et visuels -sur spectrogrammes.

tu.prop: Before starting on this phase a segmentation into phonemes is required: it was made by an expert under PRAAT on the basis of perceptual and visual clues -on spectrograms.
After possible handcorrections the transplantation of these parameters amounts to: checking that the number of segments in the two sentences of the chosen pair are equated;

tuv.seg: The analysis of phoneme durations pauses and fundamental frequency (F 0 ) is then automatic.
L'analyse de la durée des phonèmes des pauses et de la fréquence fondamentale (F 0 ) est ensuite automatique.

tu.prop: The analysis of phoneme durations pauses and fundamental frequency (F 0 ) is then automatic.
calculating lengthening or shortening coefficients for each phoneme or pause of a speaker with respect to another one;

tuv.seg: After possible handcorrections the transplantation of these parameters amounts to: checking that the number of segments in the two sentences of the chosen pair are equated;
Après éventuellement retouche manuelle la transposition de ces paramètres revient à : vérifier que le nombre de segments est égal entre les deux phrases de la paire choisie ;

tu.prop: After possible handcorrections the transplantation of these parameters amounts to: checking that the number of segments in the two sentences of the chosen pair are equated;
for each phoneme or pause building new durations which will replace the original durations of either sentence;

tuv.seg: calculating lengthening or shortening coefficients for each phoneme or pause of a speaker with respect to another one;
calculer des coefficients d'allongement ou de rétrécissement pour chaque phonème ou pause d'un locuteur par rapport à un autre ;

tu.prop: calculating lengthening or shortening coefficients for each phoneme or pause of a speaker with respect to another one;
grafting F 0 from one of the sentences onto the other one and vice versa.

tuv.seg: for each phoneme or pause building new durations which will replace the original durations of either sentence;
construire pour chaque phonème ou pause de nouvelles durées qui remplaceront les durées originales de chacune des phrases ;

tu.prop: for each phoneme or pause building new durations which will replace the original durations of either sentence;
Two new stimuli are thus obtained: the block diagram of Fig. 1 displays the different steps of the process.

tuv.seg: grafting F 0 from one of the sentences onto the other one and vice versa.
transposer la F 0 d'une des phrases sur l'autre et vice versa.

tu.prop: grafting F 0 from one of the sentences onto the other one and vice versa.

tuv.seg: Two new stimuli are thus obtained: the block diagram of Fig. 1 displays the different steps of the process.
Deux nouveaux stimuli sont ainsi obtenus : le diagramme bloc de la fig. 1 met en évidence les différentes étapes du processus.

tu.prop: Two new stimuli are thus obtained: the block diagram of Fig. 1 displays the different steps of the process.

In order to disentangle the influence of segmental vs. suprasegmental feature in the perception of Maghrebianaccented French two types of perceptual tests were run: the first one concerns French listeners (with an accent degree estimation task); the second one involves Algerian listeners (with an origin identification task).

tuv.seg: Perceptual tests
Tests perceptifs

tu.prop: The choice in favour of this second population is dictated by their geographical position in the heart of Maghreb and their familiarity with Kabyle.

tuv.seg: In order to disentangle the influence of segmental vs. suprasegmental feature in the perception of Maghrebianaccented French two types of perceptual tests were run: the first one concerns French listeners (with an accent degree estimation task); the second one involves Algerian listeners (with an origin identification task).

tu.prop: In order to disentangle the influence of segmental vs. suprasegmental feature in the perception of Maghrebianaccented French two types of perceptual tests were run: the first one concerns French listeners (with an accent degree estimation task); the second one involves Algerian listeners (with an origin identification task).

Not counting a learning phase (or more exactly of familiarisation with the type of stimuli) each test was composed of about 40 stimuli whether original or resulting from the different prosody transplantations.

tuv.seg: The choice in favour of this second population is dictated by their geographical position in the heart of Maghreb and their familiarity with Kabyle.

tu.prop: The choice in favour of this second population is dictated by their geographical position in the heart of Maghreb and their familiarity with Kabyle.

tuv.seg: Not counting a learning phase (or more exactly of familiarisation with the type of stimuli) each test was composed of about 40 stimuli whether original or resulting from the different prosody transplantations.

tu.prop: Not counting a learning phase (or more exactly of familiarisation with the type of stimuli) each test was composed of about 40 stimuli whether original or resulting from the different prosody transplantations.
The listeners all with normal hearing were not specialist in linguistics and were not paid for this task.

tuv.seg: Each experiment lasted about 15 minutes.

tu.prop: They were not urged to answer; but they could listen to each stimulus only once.

tuv.seg: The listeners all with normal hearing were not specialist in linguistics and were not paid for this task.

tu.prop: The listeners all with normal hearing were not specialist in linguistics and were not paid for this task.

The tests were conducted in the Paris region in a quiet room over headphones through the E-prime software which offers a user-friendly interface to read the instructions listen to the stimuli and capture the answers.

tuv.seg: They were not urged to answer; but they could listen to each stimulus only once.

tu.prop: They were not urged to answer; but they could listen to each stimulus only once.
Firstly native French listeners were asked to rate the degree of accentedness on a 9-point scale (from 0 to 8).

tuv.seg: The tests were conducted in the Paris region in a quiet room over headphones through the E-prime software which offers a user-friendly interface to read the instructions listen to the stimuli and capture the answers.

tu.prop: The tests were conducted in the Paris region in a quiet room over headphones through the E-prime software which offers a user-friendly interface to read the instructions listen to the stimuli and capture the answers.

0 represents standard Parisian French without any marked accent; 1 designates a very mild accent (only punctually marked); 2 a mild accent; 3 a moderate accent; 4 an average accent; 5 a rather strong accent; 6 a strong accent; 7 a very strong accent; and mark 8 designates so strong an accent that it poses intelligibility problems.

tuv.seg: Firstly native French listeners were asked to rate the degree of accentedness on a 9-point scale (from 0 to 8).

tu.prop: Firstly native French listeners were asked to rate the degree of accentedness on a 9-point scale (from 0 to 8).
The learning corpus is composed of 10 stimuli providing landmarks to assess the degree of accentedness of male and female natural voices as well as prosody modifications not used in the sequel.

tuv.seg: 0 represents standard Parisian French without any marked accent; 1 designates a very mild accent (only punctually marked); 2 a mild accent; 3 a moderate accent; 4 an average accent; 5 a rather strong accent; 6 a strong accent; 7 a very strong accent; and mark 8 designates so strong an accent that it poses intelligibility problems.

tu.prop: 0 represents standard Parisian French without any marked accent; 1 designates a very mild accent (only punctually marked); 2 a mild accent; 3 a moderate accent; 4 an average accent; 5 a rather strong accent; 6 a strong accent; 7 a very strong accent; and mark 8 designates so strong an accent that it poses intelligibility problems.
As for the test it comprises 42 randomised stimuli (18 originals + 24 French-Maghrebian crossings).

tuv.seg: The learning corpus is composed of 10 stimuli providing landmarks to assess the degree of accentedness of male and female natural voices as well as prosody modifications not used in the sequel.

tu.prop: The learning corpus is composed of 10 stimuli providing landmarks to assess the degree of accentedness of male and female natural voices as well as prosody modifications not used in the sequel.
Secondly 15 native Algerian listeners were asked to identify the origin (Kabyle Moroccan Algerian or Tunisian Arab) of the speakers they would hear.

tuv.seg: As for the test it comprises 42 randomised stimuli (18 originals + 24 French-Maghrebian crossings).
Quant au test, il comprend 42 stimuli présentés dans un ordre aléatoire (18 originaux + 24 croisements français-maghrébins).

tu.prop: As for the test it comprises 42 randomised stimuli (18 originals + 24 French-Maghrebian crossings).
The listeners who had arrived in France for 3 years on average did not know the used voices.

tuv.seg: Secondly 15 native Algerian listeners were asked to identify the origin (Kabyle Moroccan Algerian or Tunisian Arab) of the speakers they would hear.
Dans un second temps il a été demandé à 15 auditeurs algériens natifs d'identifier l'origine (kabyle marocain algérien ou tunisien) des locuteurs qu'ils entendaient.

tu.prop: Secondly 15 native Algerian listeners were asked to identify the origin (Kabyle Moroccan Algerian or Tunisian Arab) of the speakers they would hear.

The learning corpus is composed of 4 stimuli giving a sample of male and female voices of Kabyle Moroccan Algerian or Tunisian Arabian origin as well as prosody transplantations not used in the remainder of the trial.

tuv.seg: The listeners who had arrived in France for 3 years on average did not know the used voices.
Les auditeurs, qui étaient arrivés en France depuis 3 ans en moyenne, ne connaissaient pas les voix utilisées.

tu.prop: The listeners who had arrived in France for 3 years on average did not know the used voices.
As for the test it comprises 40 randomised stimuli (12 Maghrebian originals + 12 Maghrebian-French crossings + 16 inter-Maghrebian crossings).

tuv.seg: The learning corpus is composed of 4 stimuli giving a sample of male and female voices of Kabyle Moroccan Algerian or Tunisian Arabian origin as well as prosody transplantations not used in the remainder of the trial.

Le corpus d'apprentissage est composé de 4 stimuli donnant un échantillon de voix d'hommes et de femmes d'origine kabyle marocaine algérienne ou tunisienne ainsi que de transpositions de prosodie non utilisées par la suite.

tu.prop: The learning corpus is composed of 4 stimuli giving a sample of male and female voices of Kabyle Moroccan Algerian or Tunisian Arabian origin as well as prosody transplantations not used in the remainder of the trial.

tuv.seg: As for the test it comprises 40 randomised stimuli (12 Maghrebian originals + 12 Maghrebian-French crossings + 16 inter-Maghrebian crossings).
Quant au test il comprend 40 stimuli présentés dans un ordre aléatoire (12 originaux maghrébins +12 croisements maghrébins-français + 16 croisements inter-maghrébins).

tu.prop: As for the test it comprises 40 randomised stimuli (12 Maghrebian originals + 12 Maghrebian-French crossings + 16 inter-Maghrebian crossings).

tuv.seg: Results
Résultats

tu.prop: The test conducted with French listeners shows that the Maghrebian accent rated as average (about 4 out of 8) is judged as stronger after prosody modification: this artifact is due to the acoustic degradation which listeners judge even though their orders were not to concentrate on it.

tuv.seg: Test with French listeners
Test auprès d'auditeurs français

tu.prop: The weak degree (<1) attributed to the original French speakers is reassuring as for the methodology.

tuv.seg: The test conducted with French listeners shows that the Maghrebian accent rated as average (about 4 out of 8) is judged as stronger after prosody modification: this artifact is due to the acoustic degradation which listeners judge even though their orders were not to concentrate on it.

Le test mené auprès d'auditeurs français montre que l'accent maghrébin considéré comme moyen (environ 4 sur 8) est jugé comme plus fort après modification de la prosodie : cet artéfact est dû à la dégradation acoustique que les auditeurs jugent même s'ils avaient pour consigne de ne pas se concentrer sur elle.

tu.prop: The test conducted with French listeners shows that the Maghrebian accent rated as average (about 4 out of 8) is judged as stronger after prosody modification: this artifact is due to the acoustic degradation which listeners judge even though their orders were not to concentrate on it.
But above all if we compare all other things being equal a Maghrebian voice with a French prosody is judged as having a stronger accent degree than a French voice with a Maghrebian prosody.

tuv.seg: The weak degree (<1) attributed to the original French speakers is reassuring as for the methodology.
Le faible degré (<1) attribué aux locuteurs français originaux nous rassure sur la méthodologie.

tu.prop: The weak degree (<1) attributed to the original French speakers is reassuring as for the methodology.
The difference is highly significant according to a paired t-test (df=179; p<0.01) and it is more important than between original/modified Maghrebian voices (see Table 1 ).

tuv.seg: But above all if we compare all other things being equal a Maghrebian voice with a French prosody is judged as having a stronger accent degree than a French voice with a Maghrebian prosody.

Mais surtout si l'on compare toutes choses égales par ailleurs une voix maghrébine avec prosodie française est jugée comme ayant un plus fort degré d'accent qu'une voix française avec prosodie maghrébine.

tu.prop: But above all if we compare all other things being equal a Maghrebian voice with a French prosody is judged as having a stronger accent degree than a French voice with a Maghrebian prosody.
This tendency in favour of a greater role played by segmental cues is illustrated by the Maghrebians' tendency to pronounce [i] for /e/ for example in the word télévision.

tuv.seg: The difference is highly significant according to a paired t-test (df=179; p<0.01) and it is more important than between original/modified Maghrebian voices (see Table 1 ).
La différence est très significative selon un test t à deux variables (df = 179 ; p < 0,01) et elle est plus importante qu'entre les voix maghrébines originales/modifiées (voir tableau 1).

tu.prop: The difference is highly significant according to a paired t-test (df=179; p<0.01) and it is more important than between original/modified Maghrebian voices (see Table 1 ).
Formant measures confirm this fact (with F1 values in the order of 300 Hz for Algerians).

tuv.seg: This tendency in favour of a greater role played by segmental cues is illustrated by the Maghrebians' tendency to pronounce [i] for /e/ for example in the word télévision.
Cette tendance en faveur d'un plus grand rôle joué par les indices segmentaux est illustré par la tendance des Maghrébins à prononcer [i] à la place de /e/ par exemple dans le mot télévision.

tu.prop: This tendency in favour of a greater role played by segmental cues is illustrated by the Maghrebians' tendency to pronounce [i] for /e/ for example in the word télévision.
Instead if we compare F 0 targets and phoneme durations we can see that these ones are very close even between sentences which sounded to indicate a reliable effect of prosody.

tuv.seg: Formant measures confirm this fact (with F1 values in the order of 300 Hz for Algerians).
Des mesures de formants confirment ce fait (avec des valeurs de F1 de l'ordre de 300 Hz pour les Algériens).

tu.prop: Formant measures confirm this fact (with F1 values in the order of 300 Hz for Algerians).

If segmentals override suprasegmentals in what is perceived as a Maghrebian accent the question which naturally arises is to know to what extent the articulation of phonemes enables us to identify the origin within Maghreb of this accent.

tuv.seg: Instead if we compare F 0 targets and phoneme durations we can see that these ones are very close even between sentences which sounded to indicate a reliable effect of prosody.
Au contraire, si l'on compare les cibles de F 0 et les durées des phonèmes, on constate que celles-ci sont très proches même entre des phrases qui semblaient indiquer un effet fiable de la prosodie.

tu.prop: Instead if we compare F 0 targets and phoneme durations we can see that these ones are very close even between sentences which sounded to indicate a reliable effect of prosody.
Preliminary tests showed that French listeners albeit familiarised with the Maghrebian accent found it hard to distinguish Arabian and Kabyle accents: hence the follow-up test.

tuv.seg: If segmentals override suprasegmentals in what is perceived as a Maghrebian accent the question which naturally arises is to know to what extent the articulation of phonemes enables us to identify the origin within Maghreb of this accent.

Si le segmental l'emportent sur le suprasegmental dans ce qui est perçu comme un accent maghrébin, la question qui vient naturellement à l'esprit est de savoir dans quelle mesure l'articulation des phonèmes permet d'identifier l'origine au sein du Maghreb de cet accent.

tu.prop: If segmentals override suprasegmentals in what is perceived as a Maghrebian accent the question which naturally arises is to know to what extent the articulation of phonemes enables us to identify the origin within Maghreb of this accent.

tuv.seg: Preliminary tests showed that French listeners albeit familiarised with the Maghrebian accent found it hard to distinguish Arabian and Kabyle accents: hence the follow-up test.
Des tests préliminaires ont montré que des auditeurs français pourtant familiarisés avec l'accent maghrébin avaient du mal à distinguer les accents arabes et kabyle : d'où le test suivant.

tu.prop: Preliminary tests showed that French listeners albeit familiarised with the Maghrebian accent found it hard to distinguish Arabian and Kabyle accents: hence the follow-up test.
The results of the test conducted with Maghrebian listeners reveal the difficulty in identifying the origin of the stimuli taken as a whole even for Algerians (see Table 2 ).

tuv.seg: Test with Algerian listeners
Test auprès d'auditeurs algériens

tu.prop: This is at variance with the listeners' impression that they can easily recognise a Kabyle speaker for instance whose L1 is not Arabic.

tuv.seg: The results of the test conducted with Maghrebian listeners reveal the difficulty in identifying the origin of the stimuli taken as a whole even for Algerians (see Table 2 ).
Les résultats du test mené auprès d'auditeurs maghrébins révèlent la difficulté d'identifier l'origine sur l'ensemble des stimuli même pour des Algériens (voir table 2 ).

tu.prop: The results of the test conducted with Maghrebian listeners reveal the difficulty in identifying the origin of the stimuli taken as a whole even for Algerians (see Table 2 ).
Now the Kabyle origin is not better recognised than chance level (25%).

tuv.seg: This is at variance with the listeners' impression that they can easily recognise a Kabyle speaker for instance whose L1 is not Arabic.
Ceci entre en contradiction avec l'impression des auditeurs d'être capables de reconnaître facilement un locuteur Kabyle par exemple dont la L1 n'est pas l'arabe.

tu.prop: This is at variance with the listeners' impression that they can easily recognise a Kabyle speaker for instance whose L1 is not Arabic.
Only the Moroccan origin (by more than 56%) is well recognised and highly significantly better than chance (according to a 1² test df=3 p<0.01).

tuv.seg: Now the Kabyle origin is not better recognised than chance level (25%).
Or l'origine kabyle n'est pas mieux reconnue que le hasard (25 %).

tu.prop: Now the Kabyle origin is not better recognised than chance level (25%).
As for the speakers' origin with respect to their prosody it is recognised even worse.

tuv.seg: Only the Moroccan origin (by more than 56%) is well recognised and highly significantly better than chance (according to a 1² test df=3 p<0.01).
Seule l'origine marocaine (à plus de 56 %) est bien reconnue et très significativement mieux que le hasard (d'après un test du χ² de ddl=3 p<001).

tu.prop: Only the Moroccan origin (by more than 56%) is well recognised and highly significantly better than chance (according to a 1² test df=3 p<0.01).

If however we extract the speakers of each origin judged as having the strongest accent according to the French listeners we can observe better origin identification scores -even if these ones are established from smaller data.

tuv.seg: As for the speakers' origin with respect to their prosody it is recognised even worse.
Quant à l'origine des locuteurs à partir de leur prosodie elle est encore plus mal reconnue.

tu.prop: As for the speakers' origin with respect to their prosody it is recognised even worse.
The accent degrees are 4.2 for the Moroccan speaker 4.0 for the Algerian Arab speaker 7.0 for the Kabyle speaker and 4.5 for the Tunisian speaker.

tuv.seg: If however we extract the speakers of each origin judged as having the strongest accent according to the French listeners we can observe better origin identification scores -even if these ones are established from smaller data.

Si nous extrayons les locuteurs de chaque origine jugée comme ayant le plus fort accent selon les auditeurs français, nous pouvons observer de meilleurs scores d'identification de l'origine, même s'ils sont établis à partir de données plus réduites.

tu.prop: If however we extract the speakers of each origin judged as having the strongest accent according to the French listeners we can observe better origin identification scores -even if these ones are established from smaller data.

Results on the basis of their sole voices (their segmentals whatever the prosody) are very revealing (see Table 3 ): the identification scores of the Moroccan and Kabyle origins are highly significantly above chance 234456789 5 3 1 df=3 p<0.01).

tuv.seg: The accent degrees are 4.2 for the Moroccan speaker 4.0 for the Algerian Arab speaker 7.0 for the Kabyle speaker and 4.5 for the Tunisian speaker.
Les degrés d'accent sont de 4,2 pour le locuteur marocain, 4,0 pour le locuteur arabe algérien, 7,0 pour le locuteur kabyle et 4,5 pour le locuteur tunisien.

tu.prop: The accent degrees are 4.2 for the Moroccan speaker 4.0 for the Algerian Arab speaker 7.0 for the Kabyle speaker and 4.5 for the Tunisian speaker.
The Tunisian origin is also recognised with scores significantly above chance (according to a 1 ² test df=3 p<0.05).

tuv.seg: Results on the basis of their sole voices (their segmentals whatever the prosody) are very revealing (see Table 3 ): the identification scores of the Moroccan and Kabyle origins are highly significantly above chance 234456789 5 3 1 df=3 p<0.01).

Les résultats sur la base de leurs seules voix (leurs segments quelle que soit la prosodie) sont très révélateurs (voir Tableau 3) : les scores d'identification des origines marocaine et kabyle sont hautement significatifs par rapport au hasard (234456789 5 3 1 df=3 p<0.01).

tu.prop: Results on the basis of their sole voices (their segmentals whatever the prosody) are very revealing (see Table 3 ): the identification scores of the Moroccan and Kabyle origins are highly significantly above chance 234456789 5 3 1 df=3 p<0.01).
Only the Algerian Arabian origin is poorly recognised.

tuv.seg: The Tunisian origin is also recognised with scores significantly above chance (according to a 1 ² test df=3 p<0.05).
L'origine tunisienne est également reconnue avec des scores significativement au-dessus du hasard (d'après un test du χ² df=3 p<0,05).

tu.prop: The Tunisian origin is also recognised with scores significantly above chance (according to a 1 ² test df=3 p<0.05).
By and far also the prosody of these speakers having a rather strong accent when applied to various voices is not so well recognised in the confusion matrix of Table 4 below as it is in Table 3.

tuv.seg: Only the Algerian Arabian origin is poorly recognised.
Seule l'origine algérienne arabe est mal reconnue.

tu.prop: Only the Algerian Arabian origin is poorly recognised.
The same happens with Kabyle which again suggests that the very strong accent degree (7 out of 8) would be due to segmentals rather than suprasegmentals.

tuv.seg: By and far also the prosody of these speakers having a rather strong accent when applied to various voices is not so well recognised in the confusion matrix of Table 4 below as it is in Table 3.

De plus, la prosodie de ces locuteurs ayant un accent assez marqué lorsqu'elle est appliquée à diverses voix n'est pas aussi bien reconnue dans la matrice de confusion du tableau 4 ci-dessous que dans le tableau 3.

tu.prop: By and far also the prosody of these speakers having a rather strong accent when applied to various voices is not so well recognised in the confusion matrix of Table 4 below as it is in Table 3.
The Moroccan and Tunisian origins are rather well identified but the results are not (highly) significant.

tuv.seg: The same happens with Kabyle which again suggests that the very strong accent degree (7 out of 8) would be due to segmentals rather than suprasegmentals.
Il en va de même avec le kabyle qui suggère encore une fois qu'un degré d'accent très fort (7 sur 8) serait dû à des facteurs segmentaux plutôt qu'à des facteurs suprasegmentaux.

tu.prop: The same happens with Kabyle which again suggests that the very strong accent degree (7 out of 8) would be due to segmentals rather than suprasegmentals.
The Algerian Arabian origin for its part remains badly recognised.

tuv.seg: The Moroccan and Tunisian origins are rather well identified but the results are not (highly) significant.
Les origines marocaine et tunisienne sont plutôt bien identifiées mais les résultats ne sont pas (très) significatifs.

tu.prop: The Moroccan and Tunisian origins are rather well identified but the results are not (highly) significant.

tuv.seg: The Algerian Arabian origin for its part remains badly recognised.
L'origine algérienne arabe de son côté demeure mal reconnue.

tu.prop: The Algerian Arabian origin for its part remains badly recognised.
At the term of this analysis and given the achieved results segmental factors seem to be more relevant than prosody in the perception of Maghrebian-accented French.

tuv.seg: Conclusion
Conclusion

tu.prop: A fortiori segmental parameters take the first place when speakers have a strong accent.

tuv.seg: At the term of this analysis and given the achieved results segmental factors seem to be more relevant than prosody in the perception of Maghrebian-accented French.
Au terme de cette analyse et des résultats obtenus les facteurs segmentaux semblent plus pertinents que la prosodie dans la perception de l'accent maghrébin en français.

tu.prop: At the term of this analysis and given the achieved results segmental factors seem to be more relevant than prosody in the perception of Maghrebian-accented French.

Nevertheless the accent origin identification except for the strongest accents proves delicate: this is in keeping with earlier studies on the identification of Maghrebian dialects within which a considerable variability is prevalent.

tuv.seg: A fortiori segmental parameters take the first place when speakers have a strong accent.
Les paramètres segmentaux priment a fortiori lorsque les locuteurs ont un fort accent.

tu.prop: A fortiori segmental parameters take the first place when speakers have a strong accent.

Interestingly however the Moroccan accent origin is honourably recognised: better than the Kabyle origin despite a lower accent degree and although Kabyle speakers have a mother tongue which distinguishes them from other Maghrebian speakers of Arabic L1.

tuv.seg: Nevertheless the accent origin identification except for the strongest accents proves delicate: this is in keeping with earlier studies on the identification of Maghrebian dialects within which a considerable variability is prevalent.

Pour autant l'identification de l'origine des accents hormis pour les plus forts d'entre eux se révèle délicate : ceci va dans le sens d'études antérieures sur l'identification des dialectes maghrébins au sein desquels règne une grande variabilité.

tu.prop: Nevertheless the accent origin identification except for the strongest accents proves delicate: this is in keeping with earlier studies on the identification of Maghrebian dialects within which a considerable variability is prevalent.
The question this raises deserves to be addressed more thoroughly as well as the perception of Maghrebian vs. Middle Eastern accents in French with more speakers and listeners.

tuv.seg: Interestingly however the Moroccan accent origin is honourably recognised: better than the Kabyle origin despite a lower accent degree and although Kabyle speakers have a mother tongue which distinguishes them from other Maghrebian speakers of Arabic L1.

Il est remarquable cependant que l'origine de l'accent marocain obtienne des taux de reconnaissance honorables : mieux que l'origine kabyle malgré un degré d'accent moindre et bien que les locuteurs kabyles aient une langue maternelle qui les distingue des autres locuteurs maghrébins de L1 arabe.

tu.prop: Interestingly however the Moroccan accent origin is honourably recognised: better than the Kabyle origin despite a lower accent degree and although Kabyle speakers have a mother tongue which distinguishes them from other Maghrebian speakers of Arabic L1.
Finally results might be different on no longer read but spontaneous speech: it is fitting to remain cautious.

tuv.seg: The question this raises deserves to be addressed more thoroughly as well as the perception of Maghrebian vs. Middle Eastern accents in French with more speakers and listeners.
La question que cela soulève mériterait d'être creusée de même que la perception de l'accent maghrébin vs moyen-oriental avec davantage de locuteurs et d'auditeurs.

tu.prop: The question this raises deserves to be addressed more thoroughly as well as the perception of Maghrebian vs. Middle Eastern accents in French with more speakers and listeners.

tuv.seg: Finally results might be different on no longer read but spontaneous speech: it is fitting to remain cautious.
Enfin, les résultats pourraient être différents pour ce qui est du discours non plus lu mais spontané : il convient de rester prudent.

tu.prop: Finally results might be different on no longer read but spontaneous speech: it is fitting to remain cautious.

tuv.seg: Figure 1 :
Figure 1 :
Figure 1: Prosody transplantation.
Figure 1 : Transposition de prosodie.

tu.prop: Degree of accentedness judged by 15 French listeners on a 0-8 scale.

tuv.seg: Table 1 :
Tableau 1 :
Degree of accentedness judged by 15 French listeners on a 0-8 scale.
Degrés d'accent jugés par 15 auditeurs français sur une échelle de 0 à 8.

tu.prop: Degree of accentedness judged by 15 French listeners on a 0-8 scale.
Identification scores of the origin of 12Maghrebian speakers with respect to their segmentalsin row the origin of voices in column responses (%).

tuv.seg: Table 2 :
Tableau 2 :
Identification scores of the origin of 12Maghrebian speakers with respect to their segmentalsin row the origin of voices in column responses (%).
Taux d'identification de l'origine des 12 locuteurs maghrébins par rapport à leurs segmentaux - en ligne l'origine des voix en colonne les réponses (%).

tu.prop: Identification scores of the origin of 12Maghrebian speakers with respect to their segmentalsin row the origin of voices in column responses (%).
Identification scores of the origin of the Maghrebian speakers having a strong accent with respect to their segmentals.

tuv.seg: Table 3 :
Tableau 3 :

tu.prop: Percentages are given with respect to 15 listeners × 4 types of prosody per voice.

tuv.seg: Identification scores of the origin of the Maghrebian speakers having a strong accent with respect to their segmentals.
Taux d'identification de l'origine des locuteurs maghrébins ayant un fort accent par rapport à leurs segmentaux.

tu.prop: Identification scores of the origin of the Maghrebian speakers having a strong accent with respect to their segmentals.

tuv.seg: Percentages are given with respect to 15 listeners × 4 types of prosody per voice.
Les pourcentages sont donnés par rapport aux 15 auditeurs x 4 types de prosodie par voix.

tu.prop: Percentages are given with respect to 15 listeners × 4 types of prosody per voice.
Identification scores of the origin of the Maghrebian speakers having a strong accent with respect to their prosody.

tuv.seg: Table 4 :
Tableau 4 :

tu.prop: Percentages are given with respect to 15 listeners × 3 voices per type of prosody.

tuv.seg: Identification scores of the origin of the Maghrebian speakers having a strong accent with respect to their prosody.
Taux d'identification de l'origine des locuteurs maghrébins ayant un fort accent par rapport à leur prosodie.

tu.prop: Identification scores of the origin of the Maghrebian speakers having a strong accent with respect to their prosody.

tuv.seg: Percentages are given with respect to 15 listeners × 3 voices per type of prosody.
Les pourcentages sont donnés par rapport aux 15 auditeurs x 3 voix par type de prosodie.
